q: Differentiate the function $y = x^9$ with respect to $x$.
e: To differentiate $x^n$, you multiply by the power $n$ and reduce the power by one to get $nx^{n-1}$. So, for $y = x^9$, $dy/dx = 9x^8$.

q: Differentiate the function $y = x^5$ with respect to $x$.
e: To differentiate $x^n$, you multiply by the power $n$ and reduce the power by one to get $nx^{n-1}$. So, for $y = x^5$, $dy/dx = 5x^4$.

q: Differentiate the function $y = x^{12}$ with respect to $x$.
e: To differentiate $x^n$, you multiply by the power $n$ and reduce the power by one to get $nx^{n-1}$. So, for $y = x^{12}$, $dy/dx = 12x^{11}$.
Use the rule $d/dx(ax^n) = anx^{n-1}$. For $y = 4x^6$, $dy/dx = 4 \times 6 \times x^{6-1} = 24x^5$.
Use the rule $d/dx(ax^n) = anx^{n-1}$. For $y = 7x^2$, $dy/dx = 7 \times 2 \times x^{2-1} = 14x^1 = 14x$.
Use the rule $d/dx(ax^n) = anx^{n-1}$. For $y = 3x^8$, $dy/dx = 3 \times 8 \times x^{8-1} = 24x^7$.
